key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings denser and more wear-resistant, though with a slightly higher friction coefficient.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing spraying fill ratio is a practical lever for improving protective coating durability in high-friction and high-temperature machine components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratios under the same spray conditions.
Compared microstructure and phase composition using microscopy and X-ray diffraction.
Evaluated dry sliding wear in ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% coating achieved the lowest reported wear rate, 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N), and showed denser structure with low porosity.
watch_item: Results are based on a narrow set of spray ratios and dry sliding conditions, so broader operating environments may change the friction–wear tradeoff.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
